description: Drain follow-up cleanup. Closes 4 ROADMAP entries surfaced post drain-cleanup merge: P3 Trace browser filter/search; P2 UI confusion chip+button; P2 Cassandra multipart probe no-op; P3 ALLOW FILTERING denormalize. Folds the stale ralph/trace-filter cycle prep into this larger cycle. Pre-cycle State Truth Tables + Cache Invalidation Ledger + Safety Claims Preconditions sections in PRD per feedback_cycle_end_to_end.md items 8-12 discipline.

userStories.title: Server-side filter query params on /admin/v1/diagnostics/traces
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the admin diagnostics endpoint to accept filter query params and apply them in-memory before pagination so the UI can narrow the list without fetching everything.
acceptanceCriteria: Query params on `GET /admin/v1/diagnostics/traces`: `?method=` (one of PUT, GET, DELETE, POST, HEAD, OPTIONS, PATCH; case-insensitive), `?status=` (Error|OK), `?path_substr=` (free-form substring, max 256 chars), `?path=` (alias for path_substr for URL brevity), `?min_duration_ms=int`, `?max_duration_ms=int`
Empty/missing params = no filter on that axis (backwards compat preserved)
Invalid values return `400 BadRequest` with `code="InvalidFilter"` + descriptive message (e.g. `"method must be one of PUT, GET, DELETE, POST, HEAD, OPTIONS, PATCH"`)
When both `min_duration_ms` and `max_duration_ms` provided, `min > max` → 400 InvalidFilter
`internal/otel/ringbuf.RingBuffer.Filter(opts FilterOpts) []TraceSummary` applies filter in-memory before pagination

Filter logic: method matches root_name HTTP-method prefix (e.g. root_name `"PUT /bucket/key"` matches `method=PUT`); status matches `summary.Status` exact; path_substr matches `strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(root_name), strings.ToLower(query))`; min/max duration apply to `summary.DurationMs`
Total in response = filtered count (so pagination cursor walks filtered set, not full ringbuf)
OpenAPI spec updated with new query params (each with description + enum/range) + 400 response shape
Audit row preserved: `admin:GetDiagnosticsTraces`
Unit tests: each filter param independently + combinations + invalid values + min>max
Unit test: filter applied BEFORE pagination (total in response = filtered count, not full ringbuf size)
Integration test: 20 mixed traces (5 GET success, 5 PUT success, 5 PUT error, 5 DELETE error) → query with various combos → assert correct subset
`go vet ./...` passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: RingBuffer.Filter(opts) added; handler parses method/status/path[_substr]/min/max with 400 InvalidFilter on bad values + min>max; total reflects filtered count. OpenAPI updated. Unit + handler tests cover each axis, combinations, invalid values, and filter-before-pagination.

userStories.title: UI filter row in <RecentTracesPanel> + URL persistence
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a filter row above recent traces with method/status/path/duration controls and URL-persistent state so I can find a specific request fast and share the view.
acceptanceCriteria: New filter row above the trace table in `web/src/components/diagnostics/RecentTracesPanel.tsx`: Method `<Select>` (All/PUT/GET/DELETE/POST/HEAD/OPTIONS/PATCH; default All); Status `<Select>` (All/Error/OK; default All); Path substring `<Input type="search">` placeholder "Filter by path (e.g. demo-cephb)" debounced 250ms; Min duration ms `<Input type="number">`; "Clear filters" button resetting all defaults + clearing URL
Filter state synced to URL search params via react-router `useSearchParams`: `?method=PUT&status=Error&path=demo-cephb&min_duration_ms=100`; bookmark / share / browser back-forward work
Initial mount reads URL params → applies as initial filter state
Each filter change updates URL via `setSearchParams` AND triggers refetch via TanStack Query key including filter params
Empty-result state: `No traces match the current filters. Widen filters or click Clear.`
Existing Sort by Started/Duration dropdown preserved
Existing sessionStorage / localStorage history panel below live list unaffected
Reuse existing `<Select>`, `<Input>`, `<Button>` primitives
Bundle size delta ≤ 3 KiB gzipped

Playwright spec `web/e2e/trace-filter.spec.ts` covers: navigate `/diagnostics/traces?method=PUT` → only PUT traces shown; type path filter → debounced 250ms; Clear → URL clean + defaults; URL persistence via browser back/forward
Unit test: empty-filter state → no URL params written; setting filter → URL updated; reading URL on mount → state initialised
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Filter row with Method/Status Selects + path Input (debounced 250ms) + min-duration number Input + Clear button shipped above the trace table. URL persistence via react-router useSearchParams; pushState (not replace) so browser back/forward steps through filter history. Split state: filter (committed) + pathInput (typed) + debouncedPathInput effect-commit, so URL→state external nav (back/forward, paste) is immediate while typing stays debounced. Empty-filter URL writes no params (backwards compat). API client + queryKeys extended with optional RecentTracesQuery. Filtered-empty state surfaces helper text. node:test unit tests cover URL <-> filter round-trip + every axis + 18 cases. Playwright spec web/e2e/trace-filter.spec.ts covers initial URL nav, debounce, Clear, back/forward; not run locally (SSH tunnel holds :9999) — runs in CI + US-006 smoke.

userStories.title: UI clarity — rename 'Cancel deregister prep' + chip tooltip + visual hierarchy
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the cluster card after full evacuation to make clear what the primary action is (env edit + restart) and what the escape hatch is (restore to live) so the chip and button do not contradict each other.
acceptanceCriteria: `web/src/components/storage/ClustersSubsection.tsx` button when `state=evacuating + chunks=0 + deregister_ready=true`: label `Cancel deregister prep` → `Restore to live (cancel evacuation)`
Button styling: `variant="outline" size="sm"` (reduces visual weight vs primary actions)

`<DrainProgressBar>` green chip `✓ Ready to deregister` gains hover tooltip: `Edit STRATA_RADOS_CLUSTERS env to remove this cluster, then rolling restart. See operator runbook for deregister procedure.`
Card layout: chip rendered ABOVE the action button (vertical stack), not adjacent
`<ConfirmUndrainEvacuationModal>` (existing typed-confirm modal for the Restore action) body text clarified: `Moved chunks remain on target clusters; no rollback. Cluster will accept writes again.`
Playwright UI test asserts new label + tooltip text + chip-above-button layout
Bundle size delta ≤ 1 KiB gzipped
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Persist BackendUploadID.Cluster on Cassandra multipart_uploads + probe via WHERE cluster=?
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Cassandra `ListMultipartUploadsByCluster` to return the real count of in-flight multipart uploads on a cluster so the deregister_ready safety AND is fully enforced on Cassandra deploys.
acceptanceCriteria: `ALTER TABLE multipart_uploads ADD cluster text` (schema-additive — idempotent via `alterStatements` helper that swallows `column already exists`)
Multipart Init handler in `internal/meta/cassandra/store.go` captures resolved cluster id from placement picker context + persists via INSERT into the new column

Existing rows without cluster column: handler tolerates NULL value on read; backfill on first scan via `WHERE cluster IS NULL` → set to `default` (assume legacy single-cluster). Documented as one-time migration note in CLAUDE.md

`ListMultipartUploadsByCluster` Cassandra impl: `SELECT cluster FROM multipart_uploads WHERE cluster=? LIMIT ? ALLOW FILTERING` (will be replaced by denormalized lookup in US-005 — intermediate state OK for this story)
TiKV impl: verify multipart handle includes cluster in key prefix (already shipped via placement-rebalance cycle); probe scans by prefix
Memory impl: already tracks cluster in handle — verify probe returns correct count
Contract test `caseListMultipartUploadsByCluster` extension in `internal/meta/storetest/contract.go` covers all three backends with same fixture

Integration test (multi-cluster lab): open multipart Init on cephb → drain cephb evacuate → `ListMultipartUploadsByCluster(cephb, 1)` returns 1 → /drain-progress reports `not_ready_reasons=[open_multipart]` → Complete multipart → probe returns 0 → deregister_ready becomes true (assuming other conditions met)
`go vet ./...` passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: ALLOW FILTERING denormalize for GC + multipart probes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the GC queue + multipart probes to use partitioned lookup tables instead of ALLOW FILTERING so the drain hot path does not trigger Cassandra antipattern.
acceptanceCriteria: New Cassandra tables (idempotent CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS via `tableDDL`): `gc_entries_by_cluster (cluster text, region text, enqueued_at timeuuid, oid text, PRIMARY KEY ((cluster), region, enqueued_at, oid))`; `multipart_uploads_by_cluster (cluster text, bucket_id uuid, key text, upload_id text, PRIMARY KEY ((cluster), bucket_id, key, upload_id))`

Dual-write maintenance: `EnqueueChunkDeletion` writes BOTH `gc_entries_v2` AND `gc_entries_by_cluster`; `AckGCEntry` deletes from BOTH (idempotent); Multipart Init writes BOTH `multipart_uploads` AND `multipart_uploads_by_cluster`; Multipart Complete + Abort delete from BOTH
`ListChunkDeletionsByCluster` Cassandra impl: `SELECT cluster FROM gc_entries_by_cluster WHERE cluster=? LIMIT ?` — no ALLOW FILTERING
`ListMultipartUploadsByCluster` Cassandra impl: `SELECT cluster FROM multipart_uploads_by_cluster WHERE cluster=? LIMIT ?` — replaces the US-004 ALLOW FILTERING query
Lazy migration on read of legacy `gc_entries_v2` + `multipart_uploads`: dual-write to lookup table if missing (best-effort, log on failure but don't fail the read)

One-shot reconcile worker at boot: scans full legacy tables once via Cassandra paging, writes missing lookup rows; logs per-1000-rows progress for large tables + final `cluster reconcile: gc_entries=<N>, multipart_uploads=<M>, written_missing=<K>` summary; idempotent (skips already-present lookup rows). Wired in `internal/serverapp` boot path; runs once per process.

Unit + integration tests: probes hit `_by_cluster` table (asserted via query log or instrumented mock); dual-write keeps both tables consistent through random churn (insert + delete + re-insert sequences)
Memory + TiKV backends unchanged (they don't suffer ALLOW FILTERING) — verify their probes still pass after the change
Docs note in operator runbook: upgrading operators need the one-shot reconcile to populate lookup tables; reconcile runs automatically at next gateway boot; large tables (>1M rows) may take minutes
`go vet ./...` passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: E2E smoke + Playwright + docs + ROADMAP close-flip (4 entries) + PRD removal
userStories.description: As an operator and as a future-maintainer, I need every fix validated end-to-end against the multi-cluster lab + documented so I trust the cycle is actually done.
acceptanceCriteria: `scripts/smoke-drain-followup.sh` covers the 16-step walkthrough end-to-end against `docker compose --profile multi-cluster`

Smoke assertions: trace filter URL persistence (curl with query params returns subset); chip+button layout state-machine via API status; multipart-blocks-deregister flow (open multipart → drain → /drain-progress shows `open_multipart` in `not_ready_reasons` → Abort multipart → deregister_ready flips true); denormalized probes verified via query log absence of `ALLOW FILTERING`
Script EXITS NON-ZERO on any assertion failure; per-step `echo "==> Step N: ..."` lines
`make smoke-drain-followup` Makefile target wraps the script

Playwright spec `web/e2e/drain-followup.spec.ts` covers UI half: trace browser filter + URL persistence; cluster card after evacuation with new button label + chip tooltip + chip-above-button layout; multipart-blocks-deregister UI flow

`docs/site/content/best-practices/placement-rebalance.md` `Drain lifecycle` section updated: chip tooltip semantics + `Restore to live` rename rationale + multipart-blocks-deregister documented + one-shot reconcile note for upgrades
`docs/site/content/best-practices/web-ui.md` capability matrix gains rows for: Trace browser filter row, Chip-above-button layout, Multipart-blocks-deregister surfacing

`ROADMAP.md` FOUR entries close-flipped in same commit (P3 trace browser filter, P2 UI confusion chip+button, P2 Cassandra multipart probe no-op, P3 ALLOW FILTERING denormalize) — all to `~~**Px — ...**~~ — **Done.** <one-line summary>. (commit \`<pending>\`)`; closing SHA backfilled in follow-up commit on main
`tasks/prd-drain-followup.md` REMOVED in the same commit (PRD lifecycle rule)
Manual screenshots: filter row narrowing 200 traces; cluster card with new button label + chip tooltip; drain-progress JSON showing multipart-blocked deregister_ready=false
`make docs-build` succeeds
`make vet` succeeds
`make test` succeeds
`pnpm run build` succeeds
`make smoke-drain-followup` succeeds against the running multi-cluster lab
Typecheck passes
